Title: Manipulation of the brown glume and internode 1 gene leads to alterations in the colouration of lignified tissues, lignin content and pathogen resistance in wheat

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell-wall support and pathogen defence barrier
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially participates in BGI1-associated regulation of lignification and ABA signalling; direct causal effect on lignin content was not functionally tested in this paper
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flowering-stage transcriptome context; implicated alongside ABA metabolic/signalling changes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid screen and confirmation by GST pull-down, BiFC and LCI assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaPYL-1D was identified from a wheat cDNA library as a BGI1-interacting protein. 2. The BGI1–TaPYL-1D interaction was validated by Y2H, GST pull-down, BiFC and LCI assays. 3. The study proposes that this interaction may connect BGI1-dependent lignification with ABA signalling, but direct functional perturbation of TaPYL-1D on lignin was not performed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential candidate for future stacking or mechanistic studies involving ABA–lignin crosstalk, but current evidence is insufficient for direct deployment based on this paper alone
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is experimentally characterized in Results as an interaction partner relevant to nutrient/metabolite-associated lignification, but it is not demonstrated here to be a direct regulator or enzyme controlling lignin accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell-wall mechanical support and defence barrier against pathogens; contributes to stem strength/lodging resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Lignified tissues of stem internodes, especially mechanical tissue and vascular bundle; also lignified spike/glume tissues
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monolignol-forming terminal reduction step, affecting syringyl (S) unit formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cinnamaldehydes, including coniferyl aldehyde; NADPH as cofactor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamaldehydes/cinnamaldehydes to the corresponding monolignol alcohols using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms monolignol precursors required for lignin polymer deposition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important lignin-biosynthetic enzyme; key control step not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in root, leaf, stem and spike; highest in stem and spike at flowering stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Homeologs expressed highly in stem and spike; overexpression studied in stem tissues and pathogen-challenged crown/stem base tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function causes reddish-brown pigmentation in lignified tissues, reduced lignin content, reduced CAD activity and reduced stem shearing force; overexpression increases lignin content, CAD activity, S-unit-associated staining and resistance to common root rot and Fusarium crown rot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flowering stage analyses; greenhouse growth; pathogen inoculation with Bipolaris sorokiniana and Fusarium pseudograminearum under growth chamber conditions (25 °C, 16 h light/8 h dark)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; EMS mutant analysis in Ae. tauschii, tetraploid and hexaploid wheat; BSMV-sgRNA-based knockout; overexpression transgenics; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; in vivo CAD activity assay; histochemical staining; Klason lignin quantification; qRT-PCR; pathogen assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; UBI promoter for GFP localization constructs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A splice-site mutation in AET6Gv20438400/BGI1 causes the brown glume and internode phenotype in Aegilops tauschii. 2. Independent EMS and gene-editing knockouts of BGI1 homeologs in wheat confirm that loss of BGI1 function reduces CAD activity, lignin deposition and stem strength, while causing reddish-brown pigmentation in lignified tissues. 3. BGI1 encodes a cytoplasmic cinnamyl alcohol dehydrogenase with in vitro and in vivo CAD activity. 4. Overexpression of BGI1 increases lignin content and enhances resistance to common root rot and Fusarium crown rot. 5. Transcriptome and interaction assays link BGI1 to lignin/ABA-associated networks and identify TaPYL-1D as an interacting protein.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout reduces stem shearing force and lignin content; overexpression may increase lignification, so comprehensive agronomic and quality evaluation is advised although no obvious penalty was observed in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering lignin level in wheat: overexpression could improve lodging resistance and resistance to CRR/FCR; knockout/reduction could facilitate residue processing for biofuel or bio-based products. Deployment should consider target end use and possible biomass/quality tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong functional redundancy exists among A-, B- and D-genome homeologs; single or double mutants show little/no visible phenotype, whereas combined loss of all homeologs is required in hexaploid wheat and both A/B copies in tetraploid wheat. The original causal allele was identified in Ae. tauschii and validated in polyploid wheat backgrounds.
